decisions.briefing: Markets are risk-off today with NASDAQ down 1.2% and S&P 500 down 0.6%. The screener returned no candidates, so there's nothing to buy even if conditions were favorable. Holding all five current positions and keeping cash on the sidelines until momentum returns.

market_education.summary_en: Markets sold off broadly today in a classic risk-off session, with the tech-heavy NASDAQ bearing the brunt at -1.2% versus a milder -0.6% decline in the broader S&P 500. Risk-off moves typically reflect investors rotating out of growth and speculative assets into safety — cash, bonds, and defensive sectors. No specific catalysts were provided today, but the divergence between NASDAQ and S&P 500 suggests technology and high-multiple growth stocks faced the most selling pressure.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場整體呈現避險情緒，科技股為主的納斯達克指數跌幅最大，下挫1.2%，標普500指數則較為溫和，回落0.6%。市場的「避險」模式通常代表投資人將資金從成長型及投機性資產中撤出，轉向現金、債券及防禦性板塊。兩大指數的跌幅差異顯示，高估值科技股所承受的拋壓最為明顯。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Risk-off is a market mood where investors become fearful and move money away from riskier assets like stocks — especially high-growth tech — and into safer havens like bonds, cash, or gold. On risk-off days, you typically see NASDAQ fall harder than the broader S&P 500 because tech carries higher valuations and more uncertainty. As an investor, recognizing risk-off conditions helps you avoid buying into weakness and protect capital.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指市場整體氣氛趨於謹慎，投資人將資金從股票等風險較高的資產——尤其是高成長科技股——撤離，轉而流向債券、現金或黃金等安全資產。在避險日，納斯達克通常跌幅大於標普500，因為科技股估值偏高、不確定性較大。能辨別市場處於避險模式，有助於投資者避免在弱勢中追買，從而保護本金。
